meta.title: Driver Fatigue Management — <Fleet / Operation>
meta.subtitle: Hours-of-service compliance + advisory fatigue index (FMCSA 49 CFR 395 / EU 561/2006)
meta.author: HSE Logistics & Transport
meta.deid_notice: All drivers are referred to by role label. Driver medical-fitness / OSA detail is held in a separate confidential occupational-health record; identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting and the re-identification key is held separately, not part of this document.

sections.text: <Named fleet / operation / route / depot> reviewed against the resolved hours-of-service rule-set. The HOS compliance flags are COMPUTED by the fatigue.py engine from the supplied duty log, never narrated. The fatigue index is a clearly-flagged ADVISORY metric, never a regulatory threshold.
Compliance-flags-primary: the per-rule PASS/FAIL flags are the authoritative output, computed by fatigue.py (fmcsa_compliance / eu561_compliance). The fatigue index that follows is advisory only.

sections.caption: Per-rule HOS compliance — computed by fatigue.py (numbers are LOCKED engine constants; multi-day rules need the cumulative log)

sections.text: <Breach summary, if any> — the fatigue index is advisory only; the compliance flags are the authoritative finding.
Advisory fatigue index (ADVISORY / derived — NOT a regulatory threshold)

ADVISORY / derived metric — a transparent weighted-stressor heuristic (driving load + time-awake + circadian timing, peaking in the Window of Circadian Low ~02:00-06:00), NOT a regulatory threshold and NOT a validated biomathematical model. Use the compliance flags above as the authoritative output.

items: [GAP] FMCSA 60/70 h cycle & 34 h restart — not assessable from a single-shift log; collect the 7-/8-day cumulative on-duty figure (never asserted compliant from one shift).
[GAP] EU 561 weekly (56 h) / fortnightly (90 h) driving and cross-shift daily rest — request the multi-day tachograph download.

sections.text: Hierarchy-ranked controls (roster redesign first, alertness last)

rows.control: Redesign the roster / journey plan and build in rest (FRMS) — the primary control
In-cab fatigue-detection as an engineered BACKSTOP (not the headline)
Fatigue-risk rostering rules + driver fatigue briefing as administrative reinforcement

sections.text: A 'stay alert' briefing or an in-cab fatigue-detection gadget is NOT the primary control. An alertness-led treatment is a FLAG pushed up the hierarchy — fatigue is led by roster / journey-plan / built-in-rest (FRMS) redesign.

rows.action: Close the multi-day [GAP]s — collect the cumulative on-duty + weekly/fortnightly figures

items: Duty log supplied by the operator (ELD/tachograph) — a missing segment is a [GAP], never an invented value.
Multi-day cycle / restart / weekly-driving figures not supplied → [GAP]; never asserted compliant from a single shift.
India site: state return owed → [GAP] (resolve via hse-india; no national form number minted).
FMCSA Hours-of-Service — 49 CFR Part 395; EU Regulation (EC) 561/2006 + Reg (EU) 165/2014 tachograph; FRMS principles; India MTW 1961 / state rules via hse-india
Review trigger: on roster change / on ELD-exception / quarterly
Decision-support only — a competent person must review before use.
